name: the primitive type called so

VINIT.justification: The state before any ratio or count is assigned to the interaction.

TANCH.justification: The final type-checked stoichiometry value within the F3 family.

AFWD.justification: The forward transformation from a single input to a set of outputs.

AREV.justification: The reverse transformation from outputs back to inputs to verify count.

CLINK.justification: The chaining of multiple n-to-n mappings into a single stoichiometric block.

IMSCRIB.justification: Verification that the ratio n:n maintains the identity of the participating types.

FSPLIT.justification: Splitting a unified interaction into n distinct input and n distinct output channels.

FFUSE.justification: Recombining the n-channels back into a single matched stoichiometric relation.

EVALT.justification: The condition where input count exactly matches output count (n:n).

EVALF.justification: The condition where counts do not match, failing the 'so' definition.

ENGAGR.justification: A state where the ratio is simultaneously fixed and scalable across different magnitudes.

IFIX.justification: The permanent recording of the 'so' tag in the Lean environment.

phase_3.void_description: null-stoichiometry: no defined ratio or participants
phase_3.true_description: so-state: verified n:n matched many-to-many relation
phase_3.both_description: super-stoichiometric: multiple valid ratios held in superposition

steps.domain_action: initialize the stoichiometric register for the S-primitive
identify the interaction as a self-consistent computational unit
branch the interaction into parallel input and output channel sets
verify that the cardinality of the input set equals n
map the input set to the output set through the stoichiometric operator
verify the return path from the output set back to the input set
confirm the cardinality of the output set also equals n
reconstitute the parallel channels into a single matched many-to-many relation
compose the matched relation with the 'so' constructor in the S-type
fix the 'so' value in the crystal arithmetic record
close the stoichiometry definition for this instance

phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Type-level unification and implicit argument resolution
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Ordinal-based evaluation of F3 family members

phase_6.cycle_cost: Type-checking overhead and ordinal comparison cycles
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the mapping is a reversible bijection between matched sets
